value.found_quote: Claude Code uses a tiered permission system to balance power and safety: ... Bash commands | Shell execution | Yes | Permanently per project directory and command ... Claude Code supports several permission modes that control how tools are approved. ... For OS-level enforcement that blocks all processes from accessing a path, [enable the sandbox](/en/sandboxing).
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes a tiered permission system governing tool actions including Bash shell execution, with approval requirements varying by tool type (read-only vs. bash commands vs. file modification). It documents fine-grained permission rules (allow/ask/deny with wildcard specifiers), multiple permission modes (default, acceptEdits, plan, auto, dontAsk, bypassPermissions), and references OS-level sandbox enforcement for blocking all processes from accessing paths. All three elements of the claim — tiered permission system, fine-grained rules with permission modes, and OS-level sandbox enforcement — are substantiated by the page.
